about.s1_p1: The India Drought Monitor (IDM) is an interactive web portal for monitoring and forecasting drought and hydrometeorological conditions across India and its Union Territories. The portal integrates observed and forecast information on drought, rainfall, runoff, and soil moisture to support drought monitoring, early warning, and decision-making.
about.s1_p2: IDM provides weekly drought assessments based on the Combined Drought Index (CDI), which classifies conditions into Normal, Abnormally Dry (D0), Moderate Drought (D1), Severe Drought (D2), Extreme Drought (D3), and Exceptional Drought (D4). In addition to drought conditions, users can explore hydrometeorological indicators such as precipitation, runoff, and soil moisture through interactive maps, time-series visualizations, and forecast products.
about.s2_p1: The India Drought Monitor is managed by the Water and Climate Lab (WCL) at the Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar, drawing on hydro-meteorological data from the India Meteorological Department (IMD). The index is computed and reviewed each week, combining several indicators into a single drought picture for the country.
about.how_do_we_know_when_we_re_in: How do we know when we're in a drought?
about.s3_p1: Drought is not determined by rainfall alone. It develops when persistent water deficits begin to affect soils, rivers, reservoirs, vegetation, agriculture, and water supplies. To capture these different dimensions, the India Drought Monitor uses a Combined Drought Index (CDI) that integrates multiple indicators, including the Standardized Precipitation Index (SPI), Standardized Runoff Index (SRI), and Standardized Soil Moisture Index (SSMI). By combining these complementary sources of information, the CDI provides a comprehensive assessment of meteorological, agricultural, and hydrological drought conditions, offering a more robust picture of drought severity and extent across India.
about.does_the_idm_look_back_or_fo: Does the IDM look back or forward in time?
about.s4_p1: The India Drought Monitor includes both. The main map looks back in time, providing a “snapshot” of very recent conditions built on data through the previous week. Alongside it, the monitor also publishes short-range outlooks (7-, 15- and 30-day forecasts of the index), so users can see both where drought has been and where it may be heading.
about.how_does_drought_affect_the: How does drought affect the country?
about.s5_p1: Drought is a recurring and slow-onset natural hazard that can have far-reaching impacts on society, the economy, and the environment. Reduced rainfall and water availability can lower agricultural production, threaten food security, and affect rural livelihoods. Drought also places stress on domestic and industrial water supplies, reduces streamflow and reservoir storage, limits hydropower generation, degrades ecosystems, and increases the risk of wildfires and heat-related impacts. Because its effects accumulate gradually over time, drought can cause economic and social losses comparable to those associated with other major natural disasters.
about.s6_p1: The India Drought Monitor (IDM) integrates multiple datasets to provide weekly assessments of drought and hydrometeorological conditions across India. Drought monitoring is based on the Combined Drought Index (CDI), which combines precipitation, runoff, and soil moisture information. Runoff and soil moisture conditions are simulated using an ensemble of hydrological and land surface models, including VIC, H08, Noah-MP, and CWatM. Soil moisture estimates are further improved using machine learning techniques and corrected to represent root-zone conditions (0–100 cm) using SMAP Level-4 root-zone soil moisture products. Observed rainfall and temperature inputs are obtained from the India Meteorological Department (IMD), while forecast products are driven by the IMD Extended Range Forecast System (ERFS).
about.s7_intro: Want to learn more or contribute to the IDM effort? Here are some ways:
about.s7_li1: Explore the interactive map — inspect the index anywhere in India and animate the historical record on the Interactive Map.
about.s7_li2: Share local impact reports — on-the-ground observations of crop, water and pasture conditions help validate the index.
about.s7_li3: Use the data in research — the gridded indices are designed for hydro-climatic analysis; we welcome collaboration.
about.s7_li4: Get in touch with the lab — reach the Water and Climate Lab at IIT Gandhinagar with questions or feedback.

common_footer.the_india_drought_monitor_is: The India Drought Monitor is developed by the Water and Climate Lab at the Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar, drawing on hydro-meteorological data from the India Meteorological Department and the Central Water Commission, satellite products from ISRO/NRSC, and global datasets from NASA.
common_footer.strong_water_and_climate_lab: Water and Climate LabIndian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar

common_footer.water_and_climate_lab_iit_ga: Water and Climate Lab, IIT Gandhinagar

animations.watch_the_combined_drought_i: Watch the Combined Drought Index evolve week by week across the full record (July 2021 to the present). Set a date range and frame rate, then press play. Every frame is read live from that week's data grid.
animations.the_current_week_is_stamped: The current week is stamped on the map while playing.

compare.compare_two_weeks_india_drou: Compare Two Weeks | India Drought Monitor

conditions.the_combined_drought_index_b: Current-condition maps for the drought-related variables behind the Combined Drought Index — rainfall (SPI), runoff (SRI) and soil moisture (SSMI). Choose a variable and accumulation period; every layer is rendered live from the gridded data.
conditions.negative_index_values_indica: Negative index values indicate drier-than-normal conditions.
conditions.strong_spi_strong_standardis: SPI standardises precipitation; SRI standardises runoff; SSMI standardises soil moisture. The number of months is the accumulation window — shorter windows track recent conditions, longer windows reflect more persistent deficits.

contact.for_questions_about_the_indi: For questions about the India Drought Monitor, contact the Water and Climate Lab at IIT Gandhinagar using the details below. For questions about specific source data, reach out to the relevant agency partner (IMD, CWC or ISRO/NRSC).
contact.for_questions_about_the_comb: For questions about the Combined Drought Index, the weekly maps, or the
contact.for_data_access_research_col: For data access, research collaboration, or to report local drought impacts that can help validate the index, please contact:
contact.water_and_climate_lab_iit_ga: Water and Climate Lab, IIT Gandhinagar
contact.strong_how_local_reports_are: How local reports are used: Observations of on-the-ground crop, water and pasture conditions help the team interpret and validate the Combined Drought Index. Please note that a report is not an application for drought assistance, and there is no guarantee that the map will change because of any single report. The map is based on many kinds of hydro-meteorological data; local reports add valuable context about how conditions are affecting people on the ground.

data_download.all_india_drought_monitor_pr: All India Drought Monitor products are provided as plain-text gridded files. Each line is latitude longitude value on a 0.25° grid. The Combined Drought Index also has a weekly time series back to July 2021.
data_download.weekly_of_india_in_each_clas: Weekly % of India in each class (year, month, day, then class shares)
data_download.state_ut_polygon_paths_with: State / UT polygon paths with names and ids
data_download.please_cite_the_strong_india: Please cite the India Drought Monitor and the Water and Climate Lab, IIT Gandhinagar, when using these data in publications.

data_graphs.the_percent_of_india_s_area: The percent of India’s area in each drought class, every week from July 2021 to the present.
data_graphs.each_coloured_band_is_the_sh: Each coloured band represents the percentage of India’s area classified within a specific drought category during a given week (D0: Abnormally Dry through D4: Exceptional Drought). Seasonal drought expansion before the monsoon and recovery after the monsoon are visible each year, while wider dark-red bands indicate the most severe and widespread drought events.

data_query.query_the_data_india_drought: Query the Data | India Drought Monitor
data_query.run_your_own_read_only_stron: Run your own read-only SQL (AlaSQL dialect) against the same in-browser tables that power the assistant: the national weekly drought series, the latest per-state breakdown, and the monthly Hydrological Outlook means. Everything runs locally in your browser — nothing is sent to a server. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
data_query.results_will_appear_here_try: Results will appear here. Try one of the example queries on the right, or write your own.

select_from_drought_state_la;aria-label:.sql_query: SELECT * FROM drought_state_latest ORDER BY drought_pct DESC LIMIT 10

data_tables.percent_of_area_in_each_drou: Percent of area in each drought class, by state and union territory, for the selected week. These values are computed live in your browser from the Combined Drought Index grid and the state boundary grid.
data_tables.percentages_are_by_grid_cell: Percentages are by grid-cell count within each state at 0.0625° resolution.

data.a_live_stacked_area_chart_of: A live stacked-area chart of the percent of India in each drought class, every week since 2021.
data.per_state_and_union_territor: Per-state and union-territory drought percentages, computed live from the grids for any week.
data.write_your_own_read_only_sql: Write your own read-only AlaSQL queries against the drought and hydrological-outlook tables, right in your browser.

forecast_summary.intro: Pick a forecast parameter and initialization date to read the 7- to 30-day outlook, broken down by region. Download the full PDF dashboard — the 7-, 15- and 30-day forecast maps plus the regional outlook — for any available date.
forecast_summary.strong_water_and_climate_lab: Water and Climate Lab, Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar
forecast_summary.about_body: Forecast maps show the expected magnitude of each parameter accumulated (rainfall, runoff) or averaged (soil moisture) over the next 7, 15 and 30 days, and the forecast Combined Drought Index for drought. Regional figures are area summaries over North, Northwest, Northeast, East, Central, West and South India. Forecasts are driven by the IMD Extended Range Forecast System.

forecast.intro: Short-range outlooks for drought and the key hydrometeorological variables. Pick a variable and lead time; maps are rendered live from the forecast grids on the same interactive interface used across the portal.
forecast.view_outlook_summary: Read the regional forecast outlook and download the report (PDF)
forecast.about_text: Drought is the forecast Combined Drought Index. Rainfall, runoff and soil moisture show the predicted magnitude (mm, mm and v/v) over the selected lead time. Forecasts are driven by the IMD Extended Range Forecast System.

hydro_maps.individual_maps_hydrological: Individual Maps | Hydrological Outlook | India Drought Monitor
hydro_maps.these_are_the_individual_map: These are the individual maps that make up each Hydrological Outlook dashboard, shown separately so you can inspect any single month at full size. Choose a variable, then click any map to open it full-size in a new tab.

hydro_reports.pdf_reports_hydrological_out: PDF Reports | Hydrological Outlook | India Drought Monitor
hydro_reports.each_month_the_water_and_cli: Each month the Water and Climate Lab compiles the full India Hydrological Outlook into a single PDF report. Browse the archive below: pick a date to preview the report inline, or download any report directly.
hydro_reports.your_browser_can_t_display_t: Your browser can't display the PDF inline.
hydro_reports.open_it_in_a_new_tab: Open it in a new tab

hydro.the_india_hydrological_outlo: The India Hydrological Outlook (IHO) presents the latest monthly status and one-month forecast for key hydro-meteorological variables — rainfall, temperature, soil moisture, runoff and evapotranspiration — each as a percentile of the historical record. Every dashboard below shows the current month, the four preceding months, the forecast month, the same month last year, and the historically driest and wettest analogues.
hydro.see_the_individual_maps_in_t: See the individual maps in this dashboard →
hydro.each_panel_maps_a_variable_a: Each panel maps a variable as a percentile of its historical distribution for that calendar month. Low percentiles (toward red) mean the variable is well below its typical value for the month; high percentiles (toward blue) mean well above. The large panel at the top-left is the most recent observed month; the large panel at the lower-left is the one-month forecast. The two outlined panels show the historically driest (red outline) and wettest (blue outline) analogue months for context.
hydro.open_full_size_dashboard_in: Open full-size dashboard in a new tab

index.large_parts_of_peninsular_an: Large parts of peninsular and central India continue to show rainfall deficits as the pre-monsoon period advances. The Combined Drought Index indicates severe-to-extreme conditions across interior Maharashtra, northern Karnataka, Telangana and parts of Rajasthan, while improved soil moisture has eased conditions over the eastern Gangetic plains. Reservoir storage in several western and southern basins remains below the ten-year average, sustaining short-term agricultural stress.

maps.the_live_interactive_combine: The live interactive Combined Drought Index for the latest week. Hover, zoom, and jump to any state.
maps.two_live_maps_side_by_side_p: Two live maps side by side. Pick any two weeks to see how drought conditions changed.
maps.drag_a_handle_to_wipe_betwee: Drag a handle to wipe between an older week and a newer one, both rendered live.
maps.play_the_weekly_cdi_record_b: Play the weekly CDI record back through time at your chosen frame rate.
maps.render_any_week_from_july_20: Render any week from July 2021 to the present, then zoom and inspect.
maps.switch_between_the_spi_sri_a: Rainfall (SPI), runoff (SRI) and soil-moisture (SSMI) conditions at multiple accumulation periods.

slider.drag_the_handle_to_wipe_betw: Drag the handle to compare drought conditions between any two weeks.

summary.national_drought_summary_ind: National Drought Summary | India Drought Monitor
summary.strong_water_and_climate_lab: Water and Climate Lab, Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar
summary.the_india_drought_monitor_us: The India Drought Monitor uses a five-category system to identify the unusualness of dry conditions. The categories range from D0 (Abnormally Dry) through D4 (Exceptional Drought). Each weekly map represents an integrated assessment combining objective indicators and on-the-ground observations from a network of local experts.
